text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr A. Rahman
The Riverside Health Centre

12 Millbank Close
Southwark
London SE1 7QD
Dear Dr A. Rahman
Jordan Ellis, DOB: 14-05-1988, Hospital No: H739256 NHS No. 8475201639
Flat 3, 28 Hawthorne Mews, London, SE15 3AL 

Diagnoses/summary: Combined generalised and focal epilepsy, with documented focal aware auras and secondary generalisation alongside independent generalised tonic–clonic seizures. Seizure control has been stable since resection with no clinical events reported in the community, consistent with a sustained post-surgical absence of seizures. Seasonal heat exposure and prolonged exertion at work are recognised potential precipitants and are being monitored.

MRI 2019: Left mesial temporal sclerosis; postoperative MRI 2021: expected post-resection changes, no new lesion identified.

Previous investigations: Prolonged video-EEG 2020 demonstrated left temporal onset seizures and generalised spike–wave bursts independent of focal discharges. Neuropsychology 2020 pre-surgery baseline completed. Routine bloods (2024) normal including LFTs/U&Es.

Current medication: 
- Lamotrigine 200 mg twice daily
- Levetiracetam 1 g twice daily
- Folic acid 5 mg once daily
- Thiamine 100 mg once daily

Previously tried/ceased due to suboptimal control or tolerability: sodium valproate (weight gain), carbamazepine (diplopia), topiramate (cognitive slowing), clobazam (sedation), zonisamide (appetite suppression), lacosamide (dizziness).

Allergies: No known drug allergies.

Social/work context: Works outdoors in gardening/groundskeeping with variable heat exposure and prolonged physical activity, particularly in summer months. Reports good hydration practices and structured breaks; employer aware of epilepsy history and supports phased rest during heatwaves.

I reviewed Jordan Ellis in clinic today. They attended alone and provided a detailed seizure diary summary for the past 18 months. Since left anterior temporal lobectomy in March 2021, they report a durable run of seizure absence in daily life, i.e. ongoing post-operative seizure quietness without breakthrough events, including during recent hot spells when paced activity and hydration were maintained. No nocturnal events, tongue biting or post-ictal confusion described; partner has not witnessed any episodes. They previously had both focal aware auras with rising epigastric sensations and independent generalised tonic–clonic seizures prior to surgery, which supports the diagnosis of combined generalised and focal epilepsy.

They note occasional fatigue after very hot workdays but no neurological symptoms. Mood stable. No adverse effects from current medication. Weight stable. No driving, as they have chosen to remain off the road despite meeting current eligibility timeframes; this is a patient preference.

Plan:
- Continue lamotrigine and levetiracetam at current doses. No change indicated given sustained post-surgical absence of seizures in the community.
- Seizure diary to be maintained, with attention to any prodromal symptoms during peak-heat periods at work.
- Occupational advice reinforced: maintain hydration strategy, shaded rest breaks, and task rotation during high-temperature periods; patient-led and already in place.
- Blood monitoring: U&Es, LFTs, and lamotrigine level at next phlebotomy (baseline surveillance given long-term therapy).
- Safety: Night-time partner observations to continue informally; no current red flags.
- Follow-up: Routine review in 9–12 months, with open access to earlier appointment should symptoms re-emerge or if work-related heat exposure becomes more problematic.

Thank you for your ongoing care and for issuing repeat prescriptions. Please let me know if there are any concerns or if adjustments are needed between appointments.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: they report a durable run of seizure absence in daily life, i.e. ongoing post-operative seizure quietness without breakthrough events

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter repeatedly states that the patient has had no seizures since their left anterior temporal lobectomy in March 2021 and that seizure control has been stable with no clinical events reported in the community. It also notes a "durable run of seizure absence" and "ongoing post-operative seizure quietness". The clinic date is 02 October 2025, which is well over 6 months since surgery, confirming sustained seizure freedom exceeding the 6‑month criterion. Therefore, the appropriate normalized label is seizure free for multiple years.
seizure_frequency_number: they report a durable run of seizure absence in daily life, i.e. ongoing post-operative seizure quietness without breakthrough events

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for multiple year) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
